body: The exocarp forms the tough outer skin of the coffee cherry. This layer consists of a single row of strong parenchyma cells.
It functions as the ultimate biological armor against harmful external influences. Pests like the coffee berry borer are effectively kept out.
title: The Outer Protective Layer

body: During ripening, this skin undergoes a fascinating chemical change. The chloroplasts present slowly break down as the cherry ages.
Subsequently, anthocyanins take over within the cell structure. These substances provide the striking, deep red or yellow color of a ripe cherry.
title: The Color Transformation
Biological Coloring
question: Which chemical component in the exocarp's cell structure is responsible for the color change to red or yellow during ripening?

title: The Protector
question: Against which specific pest does the exocarp primarily provide a solid biological armor?
